data.short_description: Hero's Journey beat sheet for the Novel Studio app: the 12-stage monomyth cycle (Campbell, via Vogler) as an adoptable template. The stages live in this entity's lore; a novel adopts the sheet in its Blueprint, and its scenes' beat property uses these stage names exactly.

lore.title: Beats
lore.content:
The 12 stages of the Hero's Journey (Joseph Campbell's monomyth, in Christopher Vogler's writer-friendly form), in story order. Acts are rough thirds — the cycle bends freely. Scenes' beat property must use these names exactly as spelled.

Ordinary World (act 1) — the hero at home: their normal, its comforts, and the lack or wound the journey will answer. The reader must see this world to feel it left behind.

Call to Adventure (act 1) — something disrupts the ordinary: a challenge, a summons, a discovery that demands a response.

Refusal of the Call (act 1) — fear, duty, or doubt makes the hero balk. The refusal prices the journey: it shows what answering costs.

Meeting the Mentor (act 1) — a source of wisdom, training, or gifts prepares the hero. The mentor need not be a person — a map, a memory, a code will serve.

Crossing the First Threshold (act 1 → 2) — the hero commits and enters the special world, where the rules are different and the way back closes.

Tests, Allies and Enemies (act 2) — the special world tries the hero: trials teach the new rules, and the true cast sorts itself into friends and foes.

Approach to the Inmost Cave (act 2) — preparation for the central ordeal: plans, setbacks, a tightening of stakes as the hero nears the most dangerous place.

The Ordeal (act 2) — the crisis: the hero faces death — literal or of the self — and their greatest fear. Everything is earned or lost here.

Reward (act 2) — having survived, the hero seizes the prize: the sword, the secret, the reconciliation. Celebration, but with the road home still ahead.

The Road Back (act 2 → 3) — the journey turns homeward and the consequences of the Ordeal give chase; the hero recommits to finishing it.

Resurrection (act 3) — the final and most dangerous test, on the threshold of home: the hero must prove the change is permanent, dying to the old self one last time.

Return with the Elixir (act 3) — home again, transformed, carrying something that heals the ordinary world: knowledge, freedom, love, proof.
